content:
Practical guidance for “Vulnerable person privacy rule” within “Community · Psychology · Records”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Vulnerable person privacy rule” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Stop condition: Do not expose private identity, health, location, or contact details beyond genuine need. Apply this boundary to “Vulnerable person privacy rule” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Reduced fallback: If “Vulnerable person privacy rule” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.
